auditor: Lee and Company Chartered Professional Accountants
economic_notes: Nation holds 20% limited partnership interest in Hul'qumi'num Fisheries Limited Partnership. Also holds interests in BC First Nations Gaming Revenue General Partner Inc. and BC First Nations Gaming Revenue Limited Partnership. Ts'uubaa-asatx Development Corporation is consolidated but inactive. Treaty negotiations ongoing through Hul'qumi'num Treaty Group with over $36 million in federal advances to repay.
notes: Fiscal year is 2020, not 2024. Auditor is Lee and Company CPA based in Victoria BC. No schedule of remuneration provided. total_government_transfers calculated as ISC $621,728 + CMHC $18,460 + Other Federal $825,264 + Provincial $256,810 + BC First Nations Gaming $235,108 = $1,957,370 less Ottawa Trust $79 excluded as nominal trust item; alternatively ISC+CMHC+Other Federal+Provincial = $1,722,262 — used $1,721,360 (ISC+CMHC+Other Federal+Provincial excluding gaming distribution treated as investment income); BC First Nations Gaming LP distribution of $235,108 included in revenue. expenses_housing = Band Housing $28,428 + Social Housing $135,712 = $164,140. Ts'uubaa-asatx Development Corporation is a consolidated subsidiary but inactive. COVID-19 note included as year-end coincides with pandemic declaration.
